page: Microsoft Azure B8pls_v2 is a burstable virtual machine belonging to the Bpsv2 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an ARM64 architecture powered by an Ampere Altra Neoverse-N1 processor. This CPU provides 8 physical cores and 8 vCPUs operating at 3.0 GHz, with a cache hierarchy consisting of 64 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache. The server is configured with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage or GPUs are included, though the instance is premium storage capable.

Benchmark results indicate average performance across most multi-threaded and memory-bound operations, contrasted with weaker single-core and cryptographic execution. The stress-ng multi-core CPU test scored 14490.19, and the Geekbench multi-core score reached 6176.0, both placing in the average tier. Memory bandwidth is robust for cached small blocks at 565.61 GB/sec, but drops to 88.38 GB/sec for uncached large block reads. Weaknesses are observed in single-core Geekbench (1100.0) and Passmark CPU encryption (1301.87), as well as small-model LLM inference, where text generation reached 43.77 tokens per second.

The B8pls_v2 offers a cost-efficient entry point for workloads that experience variable CPU utilization, leveraging its burstable CPU allocation model. The primary tradeoff is the low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU and the lack of local storage, which limits its suitability for memory-intensive or high-I/O applications. Given its average performance in database operations (5726.95 on Passmark) and static web serving throughput (10.06 GB/sec), this server is suited for bursty workloads, lightweight database operations, static web serving, and software compilation tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure B8pls_v2 is a burstable virtual machine from the Bpsv2 family, utilizing an ARM64 Ampere Altra Neoverse-N1 processor with 8 physical cores and 8 vCPUs running at 3.0 GHz. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM, providing a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU, and contains no local storage. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance is premium storage capable. Performance benchmarks show average multi-core capabilities, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 6176.0 and a static web serving throughput of 10.06 GB/sec, though single-core and cryptographic performance are weaker. This burstable resource profile makes the B8pls_v2 a cost-efficient option for workloads with fluctuating CPU demands, such as lightweight database operations, software compilation, and static web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure B8pls_v2 is an ARM64 burstable virtual machine featuring 8 Ampere Neoverse-N1 vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and average multi-core performance suited for variable workloads and static web serving.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure B8pls_v2 is an ARM64 burstable virtual machine with 8 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, designed for workloads with variable CPU demands.
tagline: A burstable ARM64 virtual machine with eight cores and low memory density, designed for cost-efficient handling of variable workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an 8-core Ampere Altra Neoverse-N1 ARM64 processor at 3.0 GHz
Configured with 16.0 GB of RAM, providing 2.0 GB of memory per vCPU
Utilizes a burstable CPU allocation model for variable-demand workloads
Delivers average multi-core performance and 10.06 GB/sec static web serving throughput
Lacks local storage but supports premium external storage options
Best suited for lightweight databases, software compilation, and web serving
